rationale: All four exported seam signatures match the plan's pinned shapes exactly, verified by direct read of orphans.go and panel_advisory.go plus go build/vet on the three touched packages (both clean). ScanOrphanedClosedBeads(specID, workdir, excludeBeadID string) ([]Orphan, error) propagates the three infra errors; FindOrphanedClosedBeads is a thin fail-open wrapper. ClosedEpicBeadIDs(specID string) ([]string, error) wraps the same findEpicBySpecIDFn+listClosedBeadsFn pair, error-preserving. CheckPendingObligations(beadID string, getMeta func(string)(map[string]interface{},error)) error is a plain caller-supplied func; its body only calls uncoveredPendingObligations(beadID, getMeta) with zero reference to completeGetMetadataFn or any package-global, so internal/approve can inject bead.GetMetadata cleanly. PanelGateRoots(root, wtPath, specID string) []string is a one-line wrapper over unexported panelGateRoots, same return. F3-1 confirmed: uncoveredPendingObligations is the single shared core; CheckPendingObligations and reconcilePendingRefutations both call it directly, and reconcile does NOT call the erroring exported predicate. Acyclicity confirmed by grep: neither internal/lifecycle nor internal/complete imports internal/approve (only comments/doc-strings mention it), so the future approve→lifecycle and approve→complete edges stay acyclic. The PanelGateRoots discriminator caution also holds (func-scoped grep=1, bare grep in test files=2, as the plan warned).
